entry.title: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content:
The 👉 SAP Community Escape House has a new addition!!

We've built a garage for our workshop to build all kinds of stuff using SAP Build. Use your wits and knowledge about SAP Build – specifically SAP Build Process Automation – to try to escape.

WARNING: It will be really tough to escape – you have been warned! So I will be providing tips throughout the month to help you through, so subscribe to this post.

For this year, all SAP Developer Challenges created by the Developer Advocates will happen in the SAP Community Escape House (shout out to @nicoschoenteich for his amazing creativity). Each month we will unlock a new room in the house with a specific focus. Completing a challenge means successfully escaping the room by solving a series of coding – or no-code 😉 – tasks/questions. This month is all about one of the tools inside SAP Build.

Here are our previous challenges:

  • January: UI5
  • February: Integration Suite
  • March: AI

Visit the SAP Community Escape House and enter your numeric SAP Community user ID.

If you don't know your user ID, click Need Help? This will display a link to your community settings page.

Then you will see your ID here:

Once you see the SAP Community Escape House, select the SAP Build garage.

The user interface of an escape room includes the room itself as well as a terminal. You can click the objects in the room to receive tips.

Like in a real (physical) escape room, you will have to try out a few things and explore the room until you receive helpful tips and, eventually, instructions. These tips and instructions are printed in the terminal next to the room.

The terminal is also used to submit solutions to tasks. Read the instructions you receive carefully to understand how.

If you successfully escape the room (you will know when that happens), you will receive a badge in the SAP Community – and the unending esteem of your fellow SAP developers, business users and implementers. We will assign the badges once the challenge closes.

Good luck!

😺

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Report
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 (sitTokyo2025)は、SAP技術の最新状況や自身の活動・経験を紹介するエキサイティングなイベントでした。3日間にわたって開催され、多様なSAP愛好者や専門家が集まりました。
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 (sitTokyo2025) was an exciting event that showcased the latest advancements in SAP technology and provided a platform for participants to share their activities and experiences. The event was held over three days and attracted a diverse group of SAP enthusiasts and professionals.


イベント概要/Event Summary

このイベントは3日間に分けて行われ、それぞれ異なるSAPテーマとその応用に焦点を当てました。sitTokyo2025は、Connect Together, Better Together ～ ともに繋がり、ともに良くなる ～をコミュニティテーマに掲げ、今年も多くの有志によって開催をすることができました。イベント開催をサポートしてくれた運営メンバーの皆様、登壇してくれた皆様、本当にありがとうございました！ 今年のイベントでは、ユーザー企業からの登壇者が出てくれたこと、そしてJSUG (Japan SAP User Group)とのコラボ実現、新しいコミュニティメンバーの登壇と、これまでのコミュニティ活動の成果と今後の広がりを期待できる変化を感られました。これは大きな収穫でした。

The event was divided into three days, each focusing on different SAP themes and their applications.

sitTokyo2025 was held under the community theme "Connect Together, Better Together",  thanks to the efforts of many volunteers. We would like to extend our heartfelt gratitude to the organizing members who supported the event and the presenters who took the stage!  

This year's event featured speakers from user companies, collaboration with JSUG (Japan SAP User Group), and presentations from new community members, reflecting the achievements of past community activities and the promising expansion in the future.

３日間のイベントのスタッツは以下の通りです。
Here are the stats for the three-day event.

Attendee TTL: 307 / Session TTL: 16 / Event Satisfaction: 4.89 / 5.0pt

Date Track Topics
2/28 (Fri)Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP (DT) & Artificial Intelligence (AI)Attendees: 101
3/14 (Fri)Application Development and Automation (ADA) & Integration (IN)Attendees: 122
3/19 (Wed)Data and Analytics (DA)Attendees: 84

 

Sumikko さんが投稿したSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Informationにも詳細が記載されていますので、より詳しい情報や動画をみたい方はそちらをご確認ください。

For detailed information about the event, please refer to the SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Information posted by Sumikko. You can find information about the speakers and more there[^i^]. 

 

以下は各日の主なハイライトについてまとめておきます。
Below are the main highlights of each day.

Day 1

28th-Feb. Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP (DT)&Artificial Intelligence (AI)

セッションでは、SAUG-x / TechEd on tour Melbourneでの経験と学び、BTPデリバリーパートナー支援プログラム、RISE with SAPプロジェクトの悩み共有、JSUGテクニカル部会とASUG Tech Connectの紹介、Signavio Process Navigatorを使用したCloud ERPプロジェクトの推進法、ABAP開発における生成AI機能についての発表が行われました[^i^]。
The sessions included experiences and learnings from SAUG-x / TechEd on tour Melbourne, the BTP Delivery Partner Support Program, sharing challenges in RISE with SAP projects, an introduction to the JSUG Technical Committee and ASUG Tech Connect, promoting Cloud ERP projects using Signavio Process Navigator, and presentations on generative AI functionalities in ABAP development[^i^].

Day1 event youtube：http://youtu.be/1F2R8MJKv0Y

やはりAI関連のトピックスは関心が高くほぼ全てのセッションにAIに関するテーマがありましたね。内容はイベントに参加して得たSAPのAIに関する学びを帰ってから実際に試してみた話や、Jouleを日本のマーケットで利用できるようになるための情報収集から得た学びの共有、ABAP開発者の視点からのJouleへの期待、SAPのサポート部門でのAI活用事例など、多岐にわたる内容でした。それ以外にも若手の方がRISEプロジェクトで経験した学びについて共有いただいたり、Signavio Process NavigatorなどSAPが提供するプロジェクトツールの有効な活用方法などを共有いただきました。

Indeed, AI-related topics garnered significant interest, with almost all sessions featuring themes related to AI. The content ranged from sharing experiences of trying out SAP's AI learnings gained from the event, to discussing insights gathered for making Joule available in the Japanese market, expectations for Joule from the perspective of ABAP developers, and examples of AI utilization in SAP's support department. Additionally, young professionals shared their learnings from RISE projects, and effective utilization methods of project tools provided by SAP, such as Signavio Process Navigator[^i^].

登壇資料サマリー一例 /「Here are partial excerpts from session」

 


Day 2

14th-Mar.  Application Development and Automation (AD) & Integration (IN)

SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025のDay2には、Clean CoreとBTPの両立実践、SAP Event Meshで始めるイベント・ドリブン・アーキテクチャ、S/4HANA・BTP開発技術者育成の取り組み、開発者拡張とIntegration Suiteを使ったインタフェース機能の開発、SAP BPAの最新機能の紹介、生成AI活用のためのPythonアプリ開発@Cloud Foundry runtimeなどのセッションが提供されました[^i^]。
On Day 2 of SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025, sessions were provided on topics such as the practical implementation of Clean Core and BTP, starting event-driven architecture with SAP Event Mesh, initiatives for developing S/4HANA and BTP development engineers, developing interface functions using developer extensions and Integration Suite, introducing the latest features of SAP BPA, and developing Python applications utilizing generative AI on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry runtime[^i^].


Day2 Event youtube：https://youtu.be/ywP3LjdQ0FA

S/4HANA Cloudを導入し、Clean Coreを実践するために、どのような導入を意識するのか？システムの現状を可視化し最適な方法を選択するためのツールや手法を理解し、提案・デリバリできる人材を育成、BTPを利用した機能拡張・インテグレーションやビジネスAI活用のTipまで、統合的に理解できるものになっています。

To implement S/4HANA Cloud and practice Clean Core, it is essential to consider the approach to implementation. This involves visualizing the current state of the system, understanding tools and methods to select the optimal approach, and developing personnel who can propose and deliver solutions. The session covers tips for utilizing BTP for functional extensions, integrations, and business AI, providing a comprehensive understanding[^i^].

登壇資料サマリー一例 /「Here are partial excerpts from session」

 


Day 3

19th-Mar. Data and Analytics (DA)

SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025のDay3では、AIを活用したSAP Analytics Cloudの「Just Ask」機能を使ったデータモデルの可視化、SAC/Datasphereの最新アップデート情報、SAP Signavio Process Insightsを使ったS/4HANAとERP 6.0の接続事例、そしてSAP TechEd 2024（メルボルン）でのData and Analyticsに関する学びが紹介されました。特に、実際のデモや具体的な手順を含むセッションが多く、参加者にとって非常に実践的で有益な内容となりました[^i^]。

On Day 3 of SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025, sessions covered various topics including utilizing AI in SAP Analytics Cloud with the "Just Ask" feature for visualizing data models built on Datasphere, the latest updates on SAC/Datasphere focusing on planning functionalities, connecting SAP Signavio Process Insights with S/4HANA and ERP 6.0, and learnings from SAP TechEd 2024 in Melbourne centered around Data and Analytics. The sessions were highly practical and beneficial, featuring actual demos and detailed procedures[^i^].

Day3 Event youtube：https://youtu.be/lKFXUAeIGz4

コミュニティのベテランから若手、そして最も注目すべきはユーザー企業からも登壇者が出たことです。そしてその発表の内容も実際のプロジェクトに基づく素晴らしいものでとても価値の高いものでした。そしてここでもAIは重要なテーマとして取り扱われていました。

From seasoned community members to young professionals, and most notably, speakers from user companies also took the stage. Their presentations were based on actual projects and were highly valuable. AI was also treated as an important theme throughout[^i^].

登壇資料サマリー一例 /「Here are partial excerpts from session」

 

 

参加者のフィードバック / Feed Back from Audience

  • 全体的な評価 / Overall Evaluation:

    • 参加者はイベントを非常に楽しんだ様子で、「楽しかった」「最高でした」「勉強になりました」といったコメントが多く寄せられました。
    • 初めて参加した人々も「初めて参加しましたが、とても良かったです」「初参加でした、楽しかったです！」といったポジティブな感想をいただけました。
    • Participants seemed to thoroughly enjoy the event, with many comments such as "It was fun," "It was great," and "I learned a lot."
    • First-time attendees also shared positive feedback like "It was my first time attending, and it was very good," and "It was my first time, and I had fun!"

  • 学びと刺激 / Learning and Inspiration:

    • 多くの参加者がイベントを通じて新しい知識を得たことを強調しています。「勉強になりました」「新しい技術を扱えるプロジェクトに入りたい」「最新の技術キャッチアップできました」といったコメントが多く寄せられていました。
    • また、「良い刺激になりました」「技術トークはワクワクしますね！」といった感想もあり、イベントが参加者にとって刺激的であったことが伺えますね。
    • Many participants emphasized that they gained new knowledge through the event, with comments like "I learned a lot," "I want to join a project that handles new technology," and "I was able to catch up on the latest technology."
    • Additionally, there were remarks such as "It was a good stimulus," and "Tech talks are exciting!" indicating that the event was stimulating for the participants.

  • 運営と登壇者への感謝 /  Appreciation for Organizers and Speakers : 

    • イベントの運営に対する感謝の言葉も多く、また、登壇者に対しても「登壇者お疲れ様でした」「登壇緊張しましたが、楽しかったです！」といった感謝の言葉が多く寄せられています。運営として嬉しい限りです。
    • There were many words of gratitude towards the event organizers, as well as the speakers, with comments like "Thank you for the smooth operation," "Thank you to all the organizers," "Good job to the speakers," and "I was nervous about speaking, but it was fun!"

  • 次回への期待 / Expectations for the Next Event:

    • 多くの参加者が次回のイベントへの期待を示しており、「次は現地参加です！」「次は後輩連れて現地参加したいなぁ」といったコメントがありました、こんな人と人のつながりがコミュニティを強く大きくしていくんだなーと嬉しく思います。
    • また、「次の登壇ネタ作るぞ」「次は僕だ」といった意欲的なコメントもあり、参加者が次回のイベントに向けて積極的に準備を進めている様子が伺えますね。ぜひお待ちしてます！！
    • Many participants expressed their expectations for the next event, with comments like "Next time, I'll attend in person!" and "Next time, I want to bring my juniors and attend in person."
    • There were also enthusiastic comments like "I'll prepare the next presentation topic," and "Next time, it's me," showing that participants are actively preparing for the next event.

 

最後に / Wrap up

SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025は、SAPの専門家や愛好者が知識を共有し、ネットワーキングし、SAP技術の未来を探求するための素晴らしいイベントでした。魅力的なセッションと洞察に満ちたプレゼンテーションは、SAPに興味のあるすべての人にとって必見のイベントでした。

SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 was a fantastic event where SAP experts and enthusiasts shared knowledge, networked, and explored the future of SAP technology. The engaging sessions and insightful presentations made it a must-attend event for anyone interested in SAP.

イベントの詳細な内容は、Sumikko さんが投稿したSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Informationにもありますので、登壇者の情報などはそちらを参考にしてください。

For detailed information about the event, please refer to the SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Information posted by Sumikko. You can find information about the speakers and more there.

このイベントのサマリーをお楽しみいただけたでしょうか。YouTubeリンクをチェックして、セッションをフルでご覧ください。次回のSAP Inside Trackイベントでお会いできることを楽しみにしています！

I hope you enjoyed this summary of the event. Check out the YouTube links to watch the sessions in full. We look forward to seeing you at the next SAP Inside Track event![^i^]

運営メンバーの皆さん、本当にありがとうございました。皆さんの一人一人の努力・支援に支えられ今年も素晴らしいイベントになったと思います。この場をお借りして改めて感謝したいと思います。

Thank you very much to all the members of the organizing team. I believe this year's event was wonderful, thanks to the efforts and support of each and every one of you. I would like to take this opportunity to express my gratitude once again.

entry.title: Automation Alone Is Not a Strategy: Here’s Why
entry.content:
Automation Alone Is Not a Strategy: Here’s Why


Automation is revolutionizing industries, from finance to manufacturing, healthcare to retail. Companies worldwide are pouring millions into robotic process automation (RPA), AI-driven bots, and workflow automation. However, many businesses are seeing only marginal improvement  or, worse, outright failure in their automation projects.

The fundamental issue? Automation alone is not a strategy.

 Why Many Automation Projects Fail

Companies often approach automation with a narrow, tactical mindset—automating tasks instead of transforming processes. This limited view leads to inefficiencies, as automated workflows replicate existing broken processes rather than reimagining them.

For automation to deliver real value, it must be embedded in an end-to-end business transformation strategy, driven by data, intelligence, and a clear understanding of business outcomes. This is where SAP Business AI, SAP Joule, and SAP Build Process Automation come into play.

The Three Pillars of Strategic Automation

1. Align Automation to Business Outcomes
Instead of automating for efficiency alone, businesses must align automation initiatives with broader organizational goals:


✅ Customer experience: Automating customer support interactions using SAP Joule for AI-driven insights can improve response times and satisfaction levels.
✅ Revenue growth: Intelligent automation in SAP S/4HANAbcan optimize supply chains and reduce inefficiencies.
✅ Regulatory compliance: Automated auditing and reporting through SAP Business AI ensures compliance while reducing manual work.

2. End-to-End Process Transformation
Automation should not just replace human effort—it should enable new ways of working. This means:


🔹 Reevaluating workflows to remove redundant steps before automating.
🔹 Connecting enterprise applications using SAP Integration Suite to create seamless, intelligent workflows.
🔹 Leveraging AI-driven analytics to refine and optimize processes continuously.

3. AI-Driven Intelligence in Automation
The next generation of automation is intelligent automation, where AI and data analytics drive decision-making. With SAP Joule, enterprises can:


🧠 Predict issues before they arise and automate preventive actions.
🔍 Analyze vast amounts of business data from SAP Business Data Cloud to make automation smarter.
🤖 Create self-learning bots that adapt to changing business conditions.

Moving Beyond Tactical Automation

A truly strategic automation roadmap includes:


✅ Integration across enterprise applications
✅ Continuous optimization using AI insights
✅ A focus on business value, not just efficiency

Automation is a powerful tool, but without a strategy, it’s just optimization—not transformation.

How is your organization making automation more strategic? Let’s discuss!

entry.title: What's New in SAP BTP – Q1 2025
entry.content:
If you tuned in last year (or before), you probably remember our monthly bite-sized innovation highlight videos – SAP BTP Innobytes, where we showcased the coolest new features in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). Well, guess what? We’re back and better than ever in 2025, with even more enthusiasm and a demo-driven approach!

This year, we’ll be bringing you quarterly videos that dive deeper into SAP BTP innovations. While the videos will be a bit longer, they’ll be packed with demos to show these innovations in action and how they can help you achieve more.

To kick things off, we’re starting strong with our first video for Q1! Stay tuned for a closer look at all the exciting things we’ve been working on.

 

App Dev & Automation Highlights

Holistic Views Across Processes

For a holistic view across all processes, we have built dedicated Process Views for Requestors and Process Participants. These views provide information about running instances across multiple processes natively on top of Process or Workflow, without any external configuration.

Process Views has two views dedicated for Requestors and Process Participants named “My Requests” and “My Processes”.

"My Requests” application provides clarity to the Requestors on how their request fits into the workflow, the status of their request, and what actions (if any) they need to take as their request moves through the approval or fulfillment process.

The next application “My Processes” is dedicated to Process Participants like Approvers for example. Process Views help them easily track, understand, and make informed decisions about where their input is needed and how it fits into the larger workflow.

Process views are all about making complex workflows or progress status more digestible. It’s not just about having data; it’s about having the right type of view at the right time to make smarter decisions.

Watch the video demo (starting at 0:40) for more insights!

Learn more:

  • Register for the upcoming ‘What’s New in SAP Build’ webinar: https://sap.to/604200bfg

ABAP Meets SAP Build Code

Imagine seamlessly transitioning from the Cloud into the ABAP world. With SAP Build Code, that’s now a reality! In six easy steps you can integrate an ABAP remote function (RFC) into your Cloud Application Programming (CAP) project:

  1. Setup the CAP Application
  2. Explore Available Functions
  3. Add the ABAP Function to the Project
  4. Add Business Logic
  5. Implement the SAP Fiori UI
  6. Run the Application

Watch the video (starting at 2:16) to see these steps in action!

Learn more:

  • Explore SAP System Functions on the SAP Help Portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/build_code/d0d8f5bfc3d640478854e6f4e7c7584a/892114ce078b4e17a9ff7e751e6330cc.html#explore-sap-system-functions

Joule for Developers with ABAP

Are you looking for an assistant to help you build faster, smoother, and more efficiently? Look no further than Joule! In this demo (starting at 3:46), Sebastian explores how Joule can enhance your experience in ABAP Development Tools, particularly when it comes to efficient unit testing.

With Joule, you can easily identify and mock dependencies, as well as generate unit tests with minimal effort. This makes ABAP Cloud development significantly faster and more efficient, freeing you up to focus on more important tasks.

 Learn more:

  • Joule Speaks ABAP | SAP Community Blog Post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/joule-speaks-abap/ba-p/14018226

Artificial Intelligence Highlight

Reinventing Business Process with Joule and AI Agent Builder

AI agents are changing the game when it comes to business automation. These intelligent agents can plan, make decisions, and execute tasks based on user-defined goals, without the need for predefined processes. In this demo (starting at 5:06), Benjamin dives into how AI agents, powered by SAP’s Agent Builder on SAP BTP and Joule, are helping business users eliminate repetitive tasks and drive efficiency.

Learn more:

  • AI Agents on SAP.com: https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/ai-agents.html

Integration Highlight

New Adapters for Cloud Integration

Today’s enterprises operate in a complex ecosystem of applications and platforms, which makes seamless integration more important than ever. With SAP Integration Suite, we make it easy to connect with over 250 applications, not just within SAP but also with a wide range of third-party systems.

We’re excited to announce the release of four new adapters for our Cloud Integration capability, which will help businesses streamline their integration processes even further. These adapters are now available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub:

  1. IBM MQ Adapter
  2. Google Pub/Sub Adapter
  3. Azure CosmosDB Adapter
  4. Salesforce Pub/Sub Adapter

These new adapters can be easily consumed via the Integration Flow designer. The first time you select an adapter, it’s automatically imported and deployed to your system—making setup both straightforward and efficient. From there, you can quickly connect to your target systems by simply providing the necessary connection details.

Take a closer look at the video (starting at 6:56).

Learn more:

  • SAP integration Suite – ‘Connect Everything’ Blog Series: https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/sap-integration-suite-connect-everything/ba-p/13982527

Data & Analytics Highlight

Knowledge Graph Engine in SAP HANA Cloud

In today’s fast-paced world, making smart decisions requires working with highly connected, diverse, and ever-evolving data sets. SAP HANA Cloud’s Knowledge Graph Engine is here to help you tackle this challenge by improving data querying, generating deeper insights, and boosting the performance of AI agents. Explore how this powerful tool can transform the way you work with complex data in the video demo (starting at 8:02).

Learn more:

  • Connecting the Facts: SAP HANA Cloud’s Knowledge Graph Engine for Business Context SAP Community Blog Post

Cross Platform Highlight

SAP Automation Pilot Catalog for Cloud ALM Ops

The SAP Automation Pilot catalog provides instant access to ready-to-use automation commands, eliminating the need for your teams to manually execute repetitive tasks. These workflows are designed to integrate seamlessly with SAP Cloud ALM, ensuring the right context and commands are applied to the appropriate IT resources in your SAP landscape.

By automating routine operational tasks, SAP Automation Pilot significantly reduces the time spent on manual processes, freeing up valuable resources for more impactful work. Whether you’re managing incidents or performing system checks, SAP Automation Pilot helps take your SAP Cloud ALM operations to the next level.

Take a look at the demo (starting at 9:10).

Learn more:

  • “Building Technical Ops Automation Flows with GenAI (part of the learning journey: Discovering DevOps with SAP BTP )

Share your thoughts 

These videos are for YOU. We would love to hear your impressions. How do you like the video? How can we improve the upcoming videos? Please share your feedback by liking this post or leaving a comment below.

Thank you for your support and happy viewing, sharing, and enjoying!

You Might Also Be Interested In

  • SAP BTP Garage: monthly LIVE webinar with hands-on use case walkthroughs and demos.
  • SAP BTP Talk Podcast: monthly podcast covering all things SAP BTP.

entry.title: New documentation available: Extend your SAP S/4HANA Processes with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
"Intelligent Processes Are Integrated Processes"

If you're already automating and extending business processes on the SAP Business Technology Platform, you're likely thinking about the best way to connect your new process extensions to the relevant systems of record. This also includes SAP Build Process Automation, which serves as our central development environment for creating intelligent automations and orchestrating end-to-end business processes across complex IT landscapes.

The good news is that there is now a comprehensive new guide available on the SAP Help Portal to help with this process:

  • Extend and Integrate Your SAP S/4HANA Processes with SAP Build Process Automation

This guide walks you through the integration setup and management of SAP Build Process Automation with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud. It is primarily aimed at system administrators and technical consultants who support customers using SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud and wish to extend their ERP capabilities by integrating with SAP Build Process Automation.

Covering the most crucial activities for successful integration, this guide is your go-to resource for connecting SAP Build Process Automation with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

But first of all what is SAP Build Process Automation?

SAP Build Process Automation is a solution designed for citizen developers to adapt, enhance, and innovate business processes through no-code workflow management and robotic process automation.

It empowers both business users and technologists to take on the role of citizen developers. With its powerful, yet user-friendly low-code and no-code features, SAP Build Process Automation allows you to drive automation by leveraging the expertise of your citizen developer community. It allows you to:

  • Digitalize, extend, or adapt existing processes and orchestrate new end-to-end processes
  • Seamlessly leverage UiPath capabilities with SAP Build Process Automation add-on by UiPath
  • Build RPA bots to handle repetitive tasks so users can focus on higher-value goals
  • Accelerate document-heavy processes with intelligent document processing
  • Connect process and task automations to any SAP or non-SAP application

 

 What can you learn in this new documentation?

You should certainly bookmark this documentation to learn how to integrate your custom processes and automations with SAP S/4HANA, and extend standardized workflows to meet specific business requirements.

The runtime setup involves several configurations that must be executed in both the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) cockpit and the SAP Build Lobby. In this guide, we’ll walk through a common use case: pushing and retrieving business partner master data into an SAP S/4HANA system, whether on-premise or in the cloud.

Below, you can review the final architectures that you can achieve once you’ve completed the documentation. First, here’s the technical architecture for integrating SAP Build Process Automation with SAP S/4HANA on-premise:

 

Next, here’s the technical architecture for integrating SAP Build Process Automation with SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

 

 

Conclusion

It’s important to note that this documentation focuses on the integration between SAP Build Process Automation and SAP S/4HANA. However, the same approach can be applied to integrate with other SAP applications, such as SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Ariba, or SAP Customer Experience, as well as third-party systems. The setup can also be reused for processing various types of transactional or master data within a system of record. I hope this new asset will help you to simplify and standardize the connectivity o custom processes and automations to your existing backend applications.

entry.title: So this is it: I’m VERPed!
entry.content:
📢  Today is my last day working for SAP.

Indeed, I've been accepted into the very last round of the Voluntary Early Retirement Plan launched by SAP France in 2024, for which I applied the day of my 62nd birthday, on March 10.

It has been quick, but I'm happy. I thanks my management hierarchy Diana Roesner / André Borchert / Thomas Volmering / Steffen Pietsch / Michael Ameling / Muhammad Alam / Christian Klein (*) for giving me the unique opportunity to stop working 33 months earlier than expected! 😊

(*) yes, I was very low in SAP org chart 😉 but I do not care: what matters to me is to bring my expertise where and when it makes a real impact, and I humbly think it was the case during my 6.5-year tenure in this great company. 

I will really miss my wonderful team of such lovely colleagues in charge of SAP Build Product Management, Enablement and Communications 🥹  ... but I will still be hanging out here on SAP Community and on LinkedIn to follow what they will be doing with SAP Build and broadly SAP Business Technology Platform with Artificial Intelligence and Joule, because I know what will be announced in a few weeks at SAP Sapphire 2025, and for sure they will rock the stage!

 

I would like to heartfully thank Diana Roesner, André Borchert, Thomas Volmering, Michael Ameling, Sebastian Schrötel, Bhagat Nainani, Juergen Mueller and all the colleagues for having supporting me during my 6+ years at SAP, during this amazing journey from SAP Intelligent RPA to SAP Build. 🙏🏻

👩🏻‍🎓👨🏻‍🎓 I seize this opportunity to promote 4️⃣ learnings on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Build.

Indeed, I won't spoil anything but there will be tremendous announcements in a few weeks: leverage these learnings to be prepared!

✅ An Enterprise Architect’s View on SAP Business Technology Platform

✅ Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build

✅ Compose and Automate with SAP Build the No-Code Way

✅ Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation

📖  Let me also recommend the SAP Build Process Automation book from SAP PRESS!

Order this book now and learn how to create processes, automations, actions, and decisions by following step-by-step instructions with this expert-written guide by my dear colleagues @ArchanaShukla, @Chaitanya_Priya Puvvada, @Stephan Schluchter, Seshadri Sreenivas, and Steven Jacobson.

Finally, I would like to say that I'm very happy because I will spend 2 days in Paris April 14-15, 2 days in Walldorf May 12-13 and finally 2 days in Berlin May 14-15 to hang out with my colleagues and say a warm and grateful goodbye: if you will be there, come say hi! 🥲

And now, for me it's time to…

 

 💙 I wish you all the very best! 💙

entry.title: SAP Build 新功能：2025年一季度版本亮点
entry.content:
本文本为翻译文，原文档链接What’s New in SAP Build – Q1 2025 Release Highlights，原作者 Jesse Sukhbayar。

SAP Build 不断发展，利用更多人工智能、灵活性和速度，为现代团队的工作方式提供支持。2025 年第 1 季度，我们针对 SAP Build Code、Process Automation、Apps 和 Work Zone 推出了一系列创新成果，帮助开发人员和业务用户比以往更快地将创意转化为企业级解决方案。

无论您是构建自定义应用、创建 SAP S/4HANA 扩展还是简化流程，这些更新都将帮助您更高效地从概念过渡到价值。  我们来回顾一下从上一季度中选择的前 10 项更新。

 

许可和可用性更新

1.SAP Task Center 现在包含在 SAP Build Work Zone 中

SAP Task Center 现在嵌入在所有 SAP Build Work Zone 版本中，为用户提供来自多个 SAP 系统的工作流任务的统一视图 - 位于其 Work Zone 主页中。无需单独集成。此更新还扩展到合作伙伴许可，使自定义 Work Zone 场景中的 Task Center 更容易。这样，业务用户和管理人员就可以在一个工作区中加快审批速度，集中跟踪任务，并提高工作效率。

  1. SAP Build Apps 现在可在更多区域使用

现在，您可以在 EU11 和悉尼数据中心部署 SAP Build Apps，将应用程序提供给更多用户。增加的覆盖范围可提高应用的响应能力，支持区域特定的合规性需求，并为企业用例提供全局可扩展性。

 

Joule for Developers 

3.使用提示生成端到端工作流

在 SAP Build Process Automation 中，开发人员现在可以使用自然语言描述业务场景，并允许生成性 AI 自动创建表单、决策和流程流。此功能由 SAP 专门构建的大型语言模型 (LLM) 提供支持，可提供基于情境的 SAP 感知结果，从而加快工作流创建速度并减少手动工作量。它还使业务用户能够在无需编写代码的情况下对流程设计做出贡献。

4.从 SAP Build Apps 中的数据生成 UI 页面

SAP Build Apps 现在使用生成式 AI 根据您的数据模型建议用户界面页面。例如，如果您的应用包含 Books、Authors 或 Genres 等实体，Joule 会推荐相应的屏幕、布局和控件。这有助于团队快速从数据建模转变为交互式 UI 设计，缩短原型设计周期并提高设计一致性。

5.在 SAP Build Code 中使用 Generative AI 创建 Fiori UI

开发人员现在可以通过输入命令 /add-ui 或在故事板中选择焦耳图标，在 SAP 构建代码中使用 Joule 为现有 CAP 应用生成基于 Fiori 的 UI。焦耳了解您的应用程序结构，并建议相关的布局、控件和导航模式，帮助您快速使用脚手架前端应用，并专注于定制，而不是从头开始。

6.CAP 应用生成的多代理支持

SAP Build Code 现在使用由 Joule 支持的多代理支持来生成完整的 CAP 应用程序。每个代理都专注于特定部分（如数据模型、服务或 UI），因此您可以从单个提示中获取更完整的项目。此增强节省了时间，可帮助您关注业务逻辑，而不是从头开始构建所有内容。

另请阅读 SAP Build Code 中的所有 GenAI 选项概览 ， david_bizer .

 

增强 SAP Business Suite 应用程序

7.使用自定义 UI 扩展 SAP S/4HANA（计划即将发布）

即将在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（公共版）中推出，开发人员将能够使用 SAP Build 可扩展性向导创建并排扩展 - 通过定制现有 SAP Fiori 应用或构建全新的 Web 应用程序。这种方法使团队能够满足特定的业务需求，同时保留核心系统，支持精简核心原则和长期灵活性。

  • 请参阅文档：使用可扩展性向导。
  • 在设置中，请阅读博客 @Karthik-Thiru 提供的 SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 公共版。 

8.SAP Build 引导式体验的新增强功能

SAP Build 对引导式体验进行了新的增强，有助于简化复杂的流程，如员工入职、育儿假、采购审批和 IT 服务管理，其中涉及多个步骤、系统和利益相关方。 在此版本中，现在可以在 SAP Build Store 中获取 SAP SuccessFactors Employee Onboarding 和转岗的即用型内容包，从而更快地为新员工和内部调动提供一致的逐步指导。

  • 开始： SAP Build 引导式体验 ，florian_buech
  • 观看视频（2 分钟）：借助 SAP Build 引导式体验，规划育儿假。

9.实时工作流的中间消息事件

通过对中间消息事件的新支持，SAP Build Process Automation 允许工作流暂停，直到自动接收并恢复特定 API触发的输入为止。这种强大的功能可减少等待时间和手动监控，帮助团队设计更具适应性和响应能力的流程，同时减少错误和开销。 

 

提高开发人员工作效率

10.SAP Build Code 中改进的项目概览

SAP Build Code 中重新设计的项目概览提供了所有应用组件的基于树的清洁视图，支持 CAP Java 和 CAP 节点。此增强的布局与故事板集成，使开发人员能够更轻松地导航复杂项目、启用新团队成员并在构建时保持专注度。

了解更多

有关新功能的完整列表，请查看以下资源：

  • SAP Build 帮助门户
  • SAP Build Road Map Explorer

entry.title: The Death of Traditional ERP?
entry.content:
The Death of Traditional ERP?


For decades, ERP systems have been the backbone of enterprise operations, managing everything from finance and supply chain to HR and procurement. But as technology evolves, traditional monolithic ERP models are being challenged by more flexible, connected, and AI-driven solutions.

Is this the end of traditional ERP as we know it? Or is it simply evolving?

 Why Traditional ERP is Fading

Legacy ERP systems have long suffered from:
❌Rigid architectures that make upgrades costly and time-consuming.
❌Siloed data structures that limit cross-functional insights.
❌High implementation costs that deter smaller enterprises from adoption.

In today’s fast-moving business environment, companies need agility, real-time insights, and AI-driven decision-making—capabilities that traditional on-premise ERP often lacks.

 The Rise of Composable and Cloud-Based ERP

Modern enterprises are shifting to composable, cloud-based architectures that allow greater flexibility and scalability. SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a prime example of how ERP is transforming:

✅Modular and composable architecture – Companies can implement only the components they need.
✅Real-time data processing – Leveraging SAP Business Data Cloud, organizations can unify data sources for better analytics.
✅AI-Embedded Workflows – With tools like SAP Joule, ERP is no longer just transactional but predictive and automated.

How Low-Code and AI Are Reshaping ERP

The shift away from traditional ERP is further accelerated by:

🔹Low-code and no-code platforms like SAP Build, allowing business users to develop applications without heavy IT involvement.
🔹AI-driven automation through SAP Business AI, enabling proactive decision-making rather than reactive problem-solving.
🔹Connected ecosystems with SAP Integration Suite, allowing ERP to seamlessly integrate with other enterprise systems.

 What’s Next? ERP’s Evolution, Not Its End

ERP is not disappearing—it is transforming into a smarter, more connected digital core. Companies must:

✅Embrace cloud and AI to make ERP more intelligent.
✅Adopt a modular approach for greater flexibility.
✅Leverage real-time data for proactive business insights.

So, is traditional ERP dead? Not quite. It’s simply evolving into something more powerful. The real question is: Is your business evolving with it?

What’s your take? Is the ERP model as we know it coming to an end?

entry.title: Unlocking the Potential of SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath
entry.content:
The rise of artificial intelligence (AI) and robotic process automation (RPA) technologies have empowered companies to leave behind manual, error-prone, and time-consuming work for streamlined operations, customized processes, and end-to-end automated solutions. The most competitive enterprises today are developing a lasting edge by taking advantage of these capabilities.

To give our customers an even greater edge with their automation toolbox, in Q4 2024 we released SAP’s Solution Extension with UiPath to integrate with their leading RPA to automate non-SAP environments.

With the new SAP Build Process Automations integrated with UiPath, customers can extend the entire SAP enterprise automation framework, whether they be between SAP and non-SAP applications, on-premises and multi-cloud environments, and systems that span customers, employees and partners.

 

How UiPath extends the value of enterprise automation with SAP
Enterprise automation from SAP uses best-in-class SAP technologies – SAP Integration Suite, SAP Signavio solutions, AI Agents and now SAP Build integrated with UiPath – to help connect and automate complex, end-to-end business processes. These technologies work in concert to enable customers to quickly and easily connect any on-premises or cloud IT landscape, including SAP and non-SAP systems, to gain visibility into business processes and their performance, and then automate those processes seamlessly.

With this partnership, it provides SAP customers flexibility to maintain their UiPath investments while leveraging a single process orchestration layer for both SAP Build Process Automation and UiPath robots to automate non-SAP processes.  As for automating SAP processes, we continue to invest in SAP Build Process Automation and its extensive pre-build workflows and automations for SAP applications. It also serves as the foundation for Joule Studio in SAP Build that customers can use to build AI agents.

 

Fast track cloud migration with proven accelerators
The move from customized ERP systems to S/4HANA Cloud is time consuming and risky while ensuring data integrity during the complex migration process.  By leveraging SAP Activate methodology and SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath robots with proven migration content, data is accurately extracted by identifying complex code, analyze vulnerabilities, check for compatibility with S/4 and remediate any issues automatically to simplify migration efforts and prevents manual errors.

Continuous process improvement to surpass competition
Organizations face continuous changes in processes, applications and industry best practices, which can break automated strategic end-to-end business processes.  To maintain process transparency, enterprise automation with SAP portfolio includes integrated triggers that recognize inefficiencies or events, and to adjust the predetermined behavior of automation methodologies for greater productivity in S/4HANA.  To minimize disruption from application changes, SAP iPaaS and RPA can automate core processes and utilize automation techniques.  As for best practices to remain competitive, SAP is the process leader across 25 industries where knowledge is captured in industry proven benchmark and pre-built content delivered through SAP Business Accelerator hub that can be rapidly deployed.


Automate enterprise processes to optimize your business
Automating strategic end-to-end processes often require a variety of automation technologies (RPA, workflow, systems integration, APIs and events powered by AI) across heterogenous landscapes.  Enterprise automation with SAP portfolio is a Gartner market leader with profiling, iPaaS, and now RPA, which provides the process orchestration layer for comprehensive interoperability across all these automation methods.

SAP and UiPath Together: One Platform, Limitless Potential
By integrating SAP Build Process Automation workflows with UiPath’s RPA and third-party connectors, we’re offering our customers one of the largest libraries of pre-built content and accelerators to help address their comprehensive processes that span multiple applications within a complex IT landscape—regardless of the cloud software provider.  To illustrate the power of SAP and UiPath synergy, let’s examine a business scenario that shows real life automations across SAP and non-SAP environments as well as other highlights across the integrated solution.

Procurement: MRP-to-goods receipt journey
Manual interventions in the source-to-pay journey can weigh down business operations.  When processes traverse many different applications, tracking down the process exceptions or even the status of any process instance can be a laborious task requiring highly specialized skills.  Let’s take a look as to how SAP and UiPath can work together to deliver a seamless procurement journey.

The process starts with an MRP run in a manufacturing operations management solution, like Siemens Opcenter

  1. The creation of the purchase requisitions trigger the SAP Build Process Automation add-on by UiPath to create these requisitions in SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing
  2. Once in Ariba, the created requisitions are routed to the correct approver based on the SAP Build Process Automation business rules engine
  3. Approved requisitions are converted to purchase orders in Ariba and Oracle WMS is updated with the PO data for goods receipt
  4. Finally, SAP S/4HANA Cloud is updated with all relevant data to ensure prompt and accurate invoice processing.

As you can see, this process is relatively complex, yet the power of SAP and UiPath enable seamless automation across the many systems as well as a central monitoring view to be able to quickly respond to any processing exceptions.

 

Process improvement
To gain a comprehensive view of your processes across your ERP landscape, you can pinpoint bottlenecks and uncover areas with the greatest automation potential. More importantly, with contextual best practices and prebuilt content for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and other cloud solutions from SAP, your organization can receive actionable recommendations to refine its processes. For example, inefficiencies identified in SAP Signavio solutions can trigger smart actions in SAP Build—turning insights into impactful automations.

 

Enterprise-grade governance
Combine SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath’s capabilities and the comprehensive security features of SAP Business Technology Platform to help ensure compliance and trust in enterprise-wide automation efforts. 

 

Orchestrated task automation
Coordinate workflows with smooth connectivity to the SAP Task Center service, driving consistent and optimized operations.


Combined development tools
SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath combines workflow management, robotic process automation (RPA), and embedded AI into a unified low-code/no-code environment. Designed for business users and IT teams, the Automation designs originate as projects within SAP Build Process Automation, serving as the foundation for streamlined workflows and advanced process orchestration.

Key features include:

  • Integrated development tools to build business rules, approval workflows, RPA bots, and generative AI models
  • Comprehensive workflow management to orchestrate processes, monitor tasks in real time, and gain actionable insights.  Customizable process extensions help keep the ERP core clean while extending functionality with drag-and-drop tools and embedded security guardrails
  • Native integrations with SAP Integration Suite, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP Signavio solutions, and other SAP software for a unified experience
  • Cost efficiency through native embedding
    Decrease integration and maintenance costs by embedding UiPath RPA directly into SAP Build Process Automation. Unlike standalone RPA solutions, SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath eliminate the need for extensive third-party integrations.
  • Advanced monitoring for real-time insights
    This integration features authentication enabled by SAP Cloud Identity Services that gives access to UiPath services, while lifecycle synchronization helps ensure automations remain aligned across both solutions. UiPath monitoring is also fully integrated into SAP Build Process Automation, providing unified tracking and enhanced visibility into automation processes.

 

  • Digital access license is not required to connect with SAP systems
  • Streamlined design and deployment
    Use drag-and-drop tools to efficiently build and deploy automation workflows. This capability simplifies the design process and accelerates automation implementation.  For example, workflows and processes can be streamlined by leverage bots directly within SAP Build Process Automation.

    •  

Advanced automation tools

  • Comprehensive automation at scale beyond SAP solutions: Launch automation projects with thousands of prebuilt templates for third-party systems to create a unified enterprise automation ecosystem across the business.
  • AI-driven automation for smarter workflows:  Leverage built-in AI capabilities, such as intelligent document processing and predictive analytics, to enhance the efficiency of automations. Unlike many standalone RPA solutions, the SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath combines AI and automation to handle complex, unstructured data and document processing into workflows.
  • Unified automation for complex processes:  Empower your business by automating entire workflows, helping ensure comprehensive process optimization across multiple systems and reducing fragmentation in automation efforts.
  • Cloud-based bot execution:  Transition to the cloud with bots to connect with SAP solutions securely and efficiently through remote calls and surface automation using screen recordings and UI-element captures.  This connectivity supports a range of SAP user interface technologies, including SAP Fiori apps, SAPUI5, GUI, WebGUI, and line-of-business software, such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors solutions, and third-party applications. With SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath, these options provide prebuilt content, native connectors, and superior surface automation for a seamless integration experience.
  • Process optimization with advanced insights:  Leverage task mining and live insights into SAP Signavio solutions, so you can uncover inefficiencies and transform processes with actionable data.

 

How UiPath strengthens Enterprise Automation with SAP
With more than 50 years of expertise in mission-critical business processes, SAP continues to lead the way in enterprise automation.  By combining trusted solutions with the latest technologies, SAP empowers organizations to simplify operations, drive innovation, and scale transformation.

SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath are part of that long-standing legacy. By offering a clear path to unify, optimize, and future-proof your processes, the integration not only elevate automation strategies but also unlock new levels of resilience and growth.

Ready to see how enterprise automation can transform your business? Interact with our demo of SAP Build Process Automation add-on by UiPath.

 

To learn more, please check out our upcoming UiPath Solution Extension Blog Series from our SAP Product Engineering Owner, Deval Khatri, @devalk

entry.title: New Basic Trial available: Get your hands on Enterprise Automation with SAP
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business landscape, automation has emerged as a crucial competitive differentiator. While most stakeholders understand its strategic value and potential benefits, many organizations still face significant challenges on the path to becoming truly autonomous.

Enterprise Automation with SAP is the only solution that offers a comprehensive, integrated suite of tools for process mining, automation, integration, monitoring, and continuous optimization of workflows. It empowers businesses to streamline operations and drive intelligent decision-making—without adding unnecessary complexity.

 

 

With SAP Signavio, SAP Build, and SAP Integration Suite, customers gain access to best-in-class capabilities. These tools work in harmony, enabling users to quickly uncover insights, automate processes, and integrate seamlessly with existing applications to deliver the right outcomes.

Now, there’s a powerful new way to explore the full potential of Enterprise Automation with SAP. The new Basic Trial gives you hands-on access to SAP Build, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP Signavio—all in one end-to-end scenario. It’s the perfect opportunity to experience the value of automation in action. In order to activate your basic trial, you need to navigate to the Enterprise Automation webpage on SAP.com:

Access to Basic Trial for Enterprise Automation

As the next step you need to click on the ,,Start your free 30-day trial" button that will forward you to the next page.

 

 

You are now being forwarded to the Basic Trial page of SAP Build Process Automation, here you simply click on ,,Try now". This process will generate log-in credentials which you can use right away to start your hands-on tutorial.

 

 

After logging in you will be redirected to the tutorial landing page. As a last step you need to select the Enterprise Automation workbook and start your tutorial:

 

 

This includes a 2 hour hands-on exercise with 5 different units, which you can discover at your own pace:

 

 

If you want to learn more about the Basic Trial and the use case, that is being covered in this hands-on feel free to listen to our webcast, which you can find here.

 

Happy hacking!

entry.title: Introducing the New Project Creation Wizard within SAP Build Lobby
entry.content:
Introduction:

We are pleased to announce the launch of an enhanced Project Creation Wizard in the SAP Build Lobby, designed to accelerate and streamline your project initiation processes. This new tool reflects our commitment to incorporating user feedback and optimizing your interaction with SAP Build. 

Background: 

To better understand user needs, we conducted extensive research involving interviews with a diverse group of users.  

A common theme in user feedback highlighted the need for a more intuitive project creation flow, starting with the user's objective before progressing to tool selection. Users expressed interest in new capabilities directly accessible from the Lobby, such as Node.js Full Stack and Java Full Stack options. 

Different Project Creation Options

Our findings guided us in redesigning the project creation process to align with user preferences and streamline the initial steps. 

What’s New? 

  • Intent-Based Navigation: The redesigned Project Creation experience prioritizes the user's goals and required tasks. Relevant project types are grouped logically to facilitate easier and faster selection, ensuring intuitive project management. 
  • Consistent Experience: In line with Fiori UX guidelines, we offer a seamless and consistent experience across the Project Creation Wizard and Extensibility Wizard. This step-by-step approach provides clarity and guidance, helping users make informed choices regarding their unique scenarios. 
  • Modern Look & Feel: Project creation options are categorized by Objectives, Categories, and Types, assisting developers in locating the right tools promptly. This organization allows for future expansion with additional capabilities. 

Next Steps: 

We value your input and invite you to share your experience with the new Project Creation Wizard. Your feedback is crucial for continued improvement. Use the "Share Product Feedback" option and click the "Give Feedback" button to let us know how this updated experience supports your professional objectives. 

Feedback

 

For more information, please visit SAP Help Documentation. 

We look forward to enhancing your project creation journey with SAP Build and ensuring our tools meet your business needs effectively. 

Already tried it? Please feel free to share your thoughts also in the comments below!

entry.title: What Makes a Great Leader in Technology?
entry.content:
What Makes a Great Leader in Technology?


Technology leaders today face more challenges than ever. With AI, automation, and digital transformation reshaping industries, leadership in technology requires a new set of skills. Gone are the days when technical expertise alone defined success. Today, the best leaders think strategically, inspire innovation, and empower teams.

So, what separates a great technology leader from the rest?

 1. Empowerment Over Micromanagement

True leadership is about enabling others to succeed. Instead of dictating tasks, great leaders empower teams by providing:

✅ The right tools and technology – Platforms like SAP Build Process Automation allow teams to streamline workflows independently.
✅ Clear vision and autonomy – Giving employees ownership of solutions fosters innovation and accountability.
✅ A culture of trust – Teams that feel trusted are more engaged, productive, and committed.

 2. Vision Over Execution

Execution is essential, but long-term vision is what sets great leaders apart. Successful technology leaders:
🔹 Anticipate industry shifts – AI, cloud computing, and automation are changing how businesses operate.
🔹 Make data-driven decisions – Leveraging SAP Business Data Cloud enables real-time insights for smarter strategies.
🔹 Balance innovation with business impact – Technology should drive outcomes, not just projects.

 3. Adaptability Over Rigidity

In a fast-changing digital landscape, leaders must be adaptable. The ability to pivot quickly in response to new technologies, market demands, and industry disruptions is crucial. Adaptability means:
🛠 Encouraging continuous learning – Platforms like SAP Joule bring AI-driven insights that keep businesses ahead of trends.
⚙️ Embracing change as an opportunity – Viewing transformation as a growth driver rather than a disruption.
📊 Failing fast, learning faster – Experimentation and agility fuel the best technology innovations.

 The Future of Leadership in Technology

Great technology leadership is not about having all the answers—it’s about asking the right questions and guiding teams toward success. As AI and automation take center stage, leaders must:
✅ Champion strategic thinking over short-term fixes.
✅ Foster collaboration between business and IT teams.
✅ Ensure technology investments drive measurable value.

The best leaders don’t just keep up with change; they shape the future.

What leadership qualities do you think are essential in today’s technology landscape?

entry.title: Now Define Variables from Pre-existing Schemas
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps now has the ability to create page variables based on existing schemas, like from other variables, data entities, and most interestingly, processes. This is really helpful.

I especially like that for every process type of schema, you can get both the input and output schemas -- more on this below.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT: I'm sure this feature was an idea for a while, but I first heard about when the new Processes flow functions were released a year ago. It was great that you could now trigger processes from SAP Build Apps without all the manual calling of an API, but you still had to create the variables for the inputs and outputs -- if you made one data type mistake the process would fail. So the answer was for the schemas to be easily inherited from the process, and now that is possible.

The Basics

When creating a new page variable, you can still define it from scratch, but now you can simply copy the schema from somewhere else:

Important to note that these definitions copy the schema onto the new variable. You can then modify the variable definition -- there is "linking".

  • Variables: There are many times I need multiple copies of the same data -- I want to modify the data but keep the original in a different variable.
  • Actions and Process: With SAP Build Apps now able to easily call actions and processes, it is helpful to not have to create your own variables to hold the returned data, something you had to do up until now.
  • Entities: This you could do with Data Variables, but there were times when you wanted to modify that data without disturbing the original data.

Use with Processes

I got to understand the feature because I recently updated tutorials for integrating SAP Build Apps with SAP Build Process Automation, and the ability to quickly create variables made the tutorial easier to understand and quicker to perform.

https://developers.sap.com/mission.build-workshop-sales-order.html

Triggering a Process

When triggering a process, all I had to do was create a page variable from the process schema, indicating:

  1. Base it on a process
  2. Use my process
  3. Use the schemas involved with the APIs for triggering a process
  4. Use the inputs of the process

One of the pains before was that essentially in SAP Build Apps, when defining the REST APIs for calling SAP Build Process Automations, you could only have one schema definition, either the input or the output. Now, I can create page variables for the inputs and for the outputs.

Here, I set up the page variable for the inputs of our process inputs, which is quite complicated.

To trigger the process, all I had to do was bind the page variable to the fields. Then, when I click the button to trigger, I drag a Trigger Process flow function, and set the Input Parameters to the context property of the page variable. No more setting object properties and each field separately.

I can still change the binding type to Object Properties and set the fields manually, or I can use a Formula to make modifications to the page variable. 

Retrieving Instances

Getting info about the process instances was even simpler, and the ability to create both input and output schemas helped.

This time I created a variable based on the Retrieve Process Instances schemas, and used the output schema.

This provided the standard fields that are part of every such API call.

Now all I had to do was bind the page variable to the UI. And in the logic, I just put the output of the Retrieve Process Instances flow function into the variable. Really easy.

It took really no time to be able to view the list of process instances.

Tutorials

Again, you can see the whole SAP Build Apps integration with SAP Build Process Automation in the revised sales order tutorials, which are now even faster to do, less than an hour to do both the process and the app. 

https://developers.sap.com/mission.build-workshop-sales-order.html

entry.title: SAP Developer News April 24th, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

PODCAST: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/04/24/sap-developer-news-april-24th-2025/

SAP Build news

  • SAP Build included with SAP S/4HANA Cloud https://news.sap.com/2025/04/sap-build-sap-s4hana-cloud-simplify-development/
  • SAP Cloud ERP Private Package Accelerates Transformation https://news.sap.com/2025/04/sap-cloud-erp-private-package-accelerate-transformation/
  • Introducing the New Project Creation Wizard within SAP Build Lobby https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/introducing-the-new-project-creation-wizard-within-sap-build-lobby/ba-p/14079038

New Basic Trial available

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/new-basic-trial-available-get-your-hands-on-enterprise-automation-with-sap/ba-p/14075170
  • Basic trial https://www.sap.com/products/try-sap.html

Event Mesh bridge and Developer Hub

  • Event Mesh bridge in SAP Integration Suite: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/event-mesh-bridge-in-sap-integration/ba-p/14076056
  • Activating Event Mesh bridge: https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/activating-event-mesh-bridge
  • Enable SAP Developer Hub as central Event Catalog: https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/enable-sap-developer-hub-as-central-event-catalog/ba-p/14076495

 

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP Build news

2:22 New Basic Trial available

4:52 Event Mesh bridge and Developer Hub

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Into] This is the SAP Developer News for April 24th, 2025.

[DJ] In the early days of SAP R2, key developer tools were separate from the core ERP offering. When we moved to the client-server architecture of SAP R3, the key tools were built-in, part of the experience. Well, today with SAP S4 HANA Cloud, there's a return to batteries included approach because all SAP build capabilities are now included. This means that core SAP facilities for extending and personalizing SAP S4 HANA Cloud systems are right there for customers to use. Apart from a lower TCO and a simpler license model. It's basically the right tools for building extensions and integrations and ready-made content, Joule capabilities and more. It's all there from the get-go. What's more, SAP HANA Cloud is also included. Plus, you can check out all the details in the article linked in the description. By the way, this bundling is also included with SAP cloud ERP private packages and there's another article linked in the description that has more details on that and while i'm here talking about sap build there's a new project creation wizard in the sap build lobby this wizard has been redesigned based upon user research including interviews with different user groups the new design has basically a sort of a new intense based navigation rather than focusing on what tools you want to use it focuses on what you want to build it's got an updated user experience and also the categories the project categories have been reorganized to make things hopefully easier for you and the feedback doesn't stop because there's a feedback facility in the top right that you can use to send your feedback on this new wizard. So happy building and send feedback if you need to.

[Thomas] In this new segment, I'd like to tell you about the new Enterprise Automation Basic Trial. But first, a few words about what is the basic trial, particularly in the context of SAP BTP. So we have two types of trials available for SAP BTP. We have the basic trial and we have the advanced trial. Now, the advanced trial is essentially the BTP trial that you've had access to for several years now. It takes your existing SAP user account and grants it access to its own global and sub-account in SAP BTP. There you have administrative rights and the ability to set up and use many different services and capabilities of SAP PTP. You have full access to that account and can explore many different scenarios. The basic trial, by comparison, we grant you access to an existing environment. So you don't have to wait for a new environment to be provisioned and spin up and configured. You get immediate access and it isn't tied to your existing user account you're given a unique username and password with which to log in now when you access this basic trial environment it's pre-configured and set up for whatever scenario you're accessing and there's a limited number of scenarios so far but the beauty of it is that you get immediate access and certain things are pre-configured and ready for you to use. So if you're looking to try out a new capability in the easiest way possible, then the basic trial is a great way to get started. But on the other hand, if you want to dive deep and really explore a capability, the advanced trial is a better option. Now back to today's topic, which is the new enterprise automation basic trial. And this focuses on the enterprise automation scenario and includes capabilities like SAP BTP integration suite, Signavio, and SAP build business process automation. And you get all those tools and capabilities pre-configured in that environment. So if this is something that you want to explore, particularly how these different capabilities interact and work with one another, then check out this new basic trial. All the links and information are in the description below.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP Developers! So you've embraced event-driven architectures, and you're using the Event Mesh capability in SAP Integration Suite, but you're experiencing a large volume of events, and now you're looking at adopting Advanced Event Mesh to scale your event-driven architecture practice? Well, did you know that it is now possible to connect the broker part of the Event Mesh capability with your instance of Advanced Event Mesh? This is called the Event Mesh Bridge. You just configure the details of an AEM broker in the Event Mesh Bridge and set the topics that you're interested in forwarding. An AEM will start receiving all those events. Awesome, isn't it? Well, but with all those events going around in your landscape, it'll be great to have a central place to document them all, right? Fortunately, you can do this within SAP Integration Suite also. via the Developer Hub, which is part of the API management capability. You can import an async API specification, add them to a product, and make them publicly available to the developers in your organization. Check out Florian's blog post for more details. Ciao

entry.title: Simplify SAPUI5 Code Extensions with the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard
entry.content:
SAPUI5 flexibility to adapt standard SAP apps
SAPUI5 is SAP’s strategic Web framework for SAP Fiori. SAPUI5 flexibility allows developers, key users and end users to adapt standard apps with simple drag-and-drop tools. It supports a clean core approach by enabling on-stack extensions without requiring any predefined extension points. Discover all the capabilities in: SAPUI5 Flexibility - All You Need to Know.

Key user adaptation vs. Developer adaptation
In the SAP Community and many customer interactions, questions often arise about when to you use Key user adaptation vs. Developer adaptation. Key user adaptation allows business experts to change standard apps using tools directly within a running app. On the other hand, Developer adaptation targets developers who create custom code in a code-first environment using SAP Build. For a detailed comparison, check out our When to Use What guide.

The SAP Build Extensibility Wizard
SAP S/4HANA Cloud introduced the new SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, consolidating multiple extension options into one streamlined tool. No more juggling various tools or manually entering parameters such as the technical ID of the app to be extended. Instead, launch the wizard directly from a running SAP Fiori app for a simplified extension process. Learn more in: SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

What is new in SAP Build Extensibility Wizard?
The latest update to the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard introduces the ability to also create an SAPUI5 adaptation project as a User Interface Extension.

 

The wizard automatically fills the technical ID of the application to be adapted, asks for the SAP BTP Destination to connect to the SAP standard app, lets you enter the project name and off you go to SAP Business Application Studio to add your custom code.

For details see SAP Help:

  • SAP Build - Creating a User Interface Extension: SAPUI5 Adaptation
  • Details on Creating the Adaptation Project

Summary
SAP Build is the go-to platform for extending the SAP Business Suite, making it easier than ever to add custom extensions. With the new SAPUI5 adaptation project feature in the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, developers can more easily extend standard SAP Fiori apps. Start leveraging this new option today to tailor your SAP experience to what your business users need.

entry.title: From Challenge to Transformation: The Power of Intelligent Automation
entry.content:
From Challenge to Transformation: The Power of Intelligent Automation


Organizations today are facing unprecedented challenges—rising costs, increased competition, and evolving customer expectations. The answer for many? Automation. But not just any automation—intelligent automation that goes beyond basic robotic process automation (RPA) to include AI, machine learning, and real-time decision-making.

Let’s explore how businesses can leverage intelligent automation to drive real transformation.

 1. Beyond Task Automation: A Holistic Approach

Traditional RPA automates repetitive tasks, but that alone doesn’t guarantee success. True transformation requires:

✅ Reevaluating business processes – Don’t just automate; redesign workflows for efficiency.
✅ AI-powered decision-making – Use SAP Joule to analyze trends and suggest optimizations.
✅ End-to-end integration – Connecting automation across SAP S/4HANA, SAP Business Data Cloud, and SAP Integration Suite ensures a seamless workflow.

 2. Using AI to Drive Automation Efficiency
The next evolution of automation is AI-driven automation, which can:

🔹 Predict and prevent inefficiencies – AI models analyze historical data to forecast problems before they arise.
🔹 Enable self-learning workflows – Intelligent bots adjust based on performance, ensuring continuous improvement.
🔹 Enhance customer experience – AI chatbots and process automation in SAP can personalize interactions and reduce response times.

 3. A Real-World Success Story
A global manufacturing company faced challenges with supply chain inefficiencies and manual procurement processes. By implementing SAP Build Process Automation and integrating it with SAP Business AI, they:

✅ Achieved 40% operational efficiency gains
✅ Reduced procurement cycle times from weeks to days
✅ Improved decision-making with real-time insights from SAP Business Data Cloud

 4. The Future of Intelligent Automation
Automation is no longer about cost savings—it’s about scalability, resilience, and strategic growth. As businesses adopt AI and automation together, they must:

✅ Invest in AI-powered automation tools like SAP Joule
✅ Ensure seamless integration across ERP, cloud, and data platforms
✅ Continuously refine and optimize processes with real-time insights

The future belongs to companies that leverage intelligent automation to create real business value.

How is your organization using automation to transform?

entry.title: Format and Display JSON in Your App
entry.content:
I wouldn't have thought this would be a big deal, but I recently got a call from a colleague in Ireland who was building an app for an important customer. And the customer had a requirement to retrieve data and display it in formatted JSON.

So my colleague asked: How do you you display formatted JSON?

At first, we both thought you could simply use the ENCODE_JSON formula function and then display in a Text field, but that did not work. But then I discovered (with the help of a friend 😽) that that function has a parameter, and that is all you need.

How to Do It

To demonstrate, I connected to the Northwind Product entity.

I then created a data variable that returned the first product, again, just to have some data.

On my UI, I created a container with a text box, whose content was set to a page variable, textToDisplay (with an initial value). I used the built-in style Primary Card to make it look nice.

SIDE NOTE: I used the custom width/height setting to make the container a minimum size but let it expand with the data. 

I then added a Set Page Variable in the data variable logic to set the page variable.

And I set the page variable to the formula below. Note some interesting things:

  • If you set the Spaces parameter of ENCODE_JSON, you will get the JSON nicely formatted.
  • Again, notice the formula editor has really good documentation on every function (if I would only read it 🤦).
ENCODE_JSON(data.Products1,4)

The result?

Without the Spaces parameter, it would have displayed like this:

Making It Editable

You could also put the text in an input box so a user could edit it.

Then you could provide a button to validate the JSON they type by using a simple JavaScript flow function.

I set the input to the page variable, changed the output to true/false, and used the following code:

If I click Validate with the default JSON:

If I make an error:

👉 Let me know if this was helpful, and maybe how you would improve on this.

entry.title: SAP BTP SDK for Android 25.4.0 is now available
entry.content:
Release announcement: SAP BTP SDK for Android 25.4.0 can now be downloaded

 

 

The new version of SAP BTP SDK for Android – version 25.4.0 is now available.

 

You can download the latest version of the SDK either from the Software Center or the downloads section on develeopers.sap.com

The SAP BTP SDK for Android team brings some exciting new features to you with this new release.

In this blog, we will highlight some of the cool features that come with the new upgrade. To get a detailed overview of all the features released, please visit our user documentation and our API reference documentation.

 

Highlights

 

Wizard & Version Info

SAP BTP SDK for Android 25.4 requires Android Studio 2024.3.1 or later.

Additionally, the Wizard now fully supports Complex OData Types in the scaffolding applications it generates. Whether you're using the generated app as a reference or a starting point, it will handle OData services with complex types, simplifying the development of UIs based on complex data structures.

 

SAP Fiori

As with every release, version 25.4 brings exciting enhancements to the Fiori UI Libraries.

The Jetpack Compose library has new and enhanced components, including support for laser scanners, enhanced Rating Controls, and a redesigned Collection View. The Date Picker now integrates with the Fiori Compose Calendar, offering features like year selection, date range selection, and the ability to clear selected dates. Additionally, AI Progress Indicators and Banner control updates bring real-time feedback and support for multiple error messages, improving the user experience.

For View-Based components, the Inline Signature Capture now includes mandatory field indicators, while the Object Cell component supports multiline text for more flexible content display. The Banner component can now display multiple error messages, and a new inline notice component has been introduced to inform users when content is AI-generated, promoting transparency and trust.

 

Foundation

In SAP BTP SDK for Android 25.4, significant updates have been made. A key enhancement is the introduction of a digits-only passcode policy, which allows admins to enforce numeric-only passcodes, along with displaying a digits-only keypad to streamline user interactions. Additionally, a new passcode history policy can be configured by the admins to define how many previous passcodes a user cannot reuse when resetting their passcode. The SDK also sees an upgrade to the Baidu library, now at version 8.3.68.0, ensuring continued support for location-based services and map-related functionality.

 

OData

The Offline OData Library has also been enhanced in this release. It now supports Crossjoin Data Aggregation, making it easier to perform complex queries in offline scenarios. Furthermore, the UNBIND operation has been added to the UpdateEntity requests, providing more flexibility when managing entity relationships offline.

 

Raise A Request

If you have feature ideas that the SDK should support, please raise an improvement request here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/2279.

 

Feature Summary

This blog highlights some of the features released with the new version of SAP BTP SDK for Android – 25.4.0. For details, please check the important links section.

  • Support for digits-only passcodes and enhanced admin-enforced passcode history policies
  • New Fiori controls such as AI Notice and AI Progress Indicators, along with enhancements to other controls such as Date Picker and Banner
  • Baidu library upgrade to version 8.3.68.0
  • Support for crossjoin data aggregation and the UNBIND operation in Offline OData

 

Important Links

  • SAP BTP SDK for Android 25.4.0 (Software Center)
  • Documentation
  • API Reference

 

Acknowledgement

A huge kudos to the spectacular team that worked tirelessly, right from conceptualizing the improvements, to defining, designing, building, and eventually delivering them.

entry.title: SAP BTP SDK for iOS 25.4.0 is now available
entry.content:
Release announcement: SAP BTP SDK for iOS 25.4.0 can be downloaded

 

The new version of SAP BTP SDK for iOS 25.4.0 is now generally available.

 

You can download the latest version of the SDK either from the Software Center or the downloads section on develeopers.sap.com

With this latest release, the SAP BTP SDK for iOS team has brought some exciting new features for you to explore.

In this blog, we will highlight the new upgrade's offerings. Please visit our documentation and API reference to get a detailed overview of all the features released.

 

Highlights

 

Version Information

With version 25.4, the SAP BTP SDK for iOS supports iOS 17 and higher versions and requires Xcode 16.2 or newer for app development.

 

SAP Fiori

As with every release, version 25.4 brings a number of important Fiori enhancements. The new AI Notice component provides a mechanism for developers to transparently indicate when AI-generated content is presented, helping to build user trust. Additionally, the new AI Progress Indicator provides an icon and a descriptive message to offer users clear context during AI-driven processes. Toast messages have been enhanced to deliver a smoother and more consistent notification experience. The FUIAttachmentViewController now clearly indicates which attachments are mandatory and can be configured to display a confirmation dialog when users attempt to delete attachments.

 

Foundation Enhancements

On the foundation side, developers will find new capabilities that improve diagnostics and strengthen user security. Applications can now export local client logs without requiring user authentication, making it easier to troubleshoot issues before onboarding is complete. Additionally, administrators can now enforce a digits-only passcode policy, which streamlines security compliance and enhances the corporate user experience by automatically displaying a digits-only keyboard.

 

OData

Offline functionality continues to be a strong focus area. In this release, Offline OData has been enhanced to support crossjoin data aggregation, making it easier to perform complex offline queries. Additionally, support for the UNBIND operation in UpdateEntity requests has been added, allowing developers greater flexibility when managing entity relationships in offline scenarios.

 

Raise A Request

If you have feature ideas that the SDK should support, please raise an improvement request here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/2279.

 

Feature Summary

This blog highlights some of the key features introduced in SAP BTP SDK for iOS 25.4.0. For more detailed information, please refer to the important links section below.

  • Support for Xcode 16.2 and iOS 17+
  • Digits-only passcode support
  • New Fiori Controls like AI Notice and Progress Indicator
  • Crossjoin Data Aggregation and UNBIND operation in UpdateEntity

 

Important Links

  • SAP BTP SDK for iOS 25.4 Download (Software Center)
  • Documentation
  • API Reference

 

Acknowledgement

A huge kudos to the spectacular team that worked tirelessly, right from conceptualizing the improvements, to defining, designing, building, and eventually delivering them.

entry.title: Introducing the New Pricing and Packaging for SAP Build: A Unified Experience for all Developers
entry.content:
As SAP is evolving its Business Suite, AI, and Data Products to meet rising market demands, and as part of this strategy, we are introducing a new exciting, unified commercial model for SAP Build. This updated offering consolidates capabilities for pro-code, low code and AI powered development tools making it easier than ever to build applications and extensions within the SAP ecosystem.

With built-in access to runtime, automation, and extensibility services, SAP Build now empowers both business users and professional developers to choose the right tool set for the right job. The integration of generative AI enhances productivity by streamlining workflows, automating tasks, and accelerating decision-making.

To further support development, SAP Build capabilities along with SAP HANA Cloud are now fully included in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Packages empowering customers to handle the majority of their expected scenarios right out of the box. Read the full announcement here.

What changes?

We've heard our customers loud and clear; they asked for simpler licensing, easier provisioning, and a unified toolset. Previously, each SAP Build product like Build Apps, Process Automation, Work Zone, Build Code, and ABAP was licensed separately. This resulted in having lots of different services for customers to manage.  

The following list of 14 services from SAP Build have now been consolidated:

SAP Build Services:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
